one_line_positioning: AI Product Engineer who builds intelligence layers from concept to production
narrative_angle: Backend AI engineer with 2 years of production LLM expertise, focused on building the intelligence core for customer personalization and clienteling tools; eager to adapt to design-led retail environments.
lead_strengths: LLM API production experience (multi-model routing, tool-calling)
End-to-end AI system shipping (0 to 450+ users)
Cost and latency optimization (98% cost reduction)
Self-starter ownership (co-founder, built product from scratch)
proof_points_to_emphasize: Built consumer AI companion with 13–15% weekly retention and 98% cost reduction
Shipped enterprise AI system improving quality workflow consistency and reducing latency 36%
Automated user acquisition via personalized bot, increasing activation from 20% to 75%
things_to_downplay: No direct CEO-facing or design team collaboration
tone_guidance: Confident in technical AI execution, honest about learning edges in frontend and retail, business-outcome focused.
recruiter_first_impression_target: A product-minded AI engineer who thinks in business outcomes and can own the intelligence layer end-to-end, despite needing ramp-up on frontend and retail domain.
reasoning: Strong AI engineering match with production LLM, Python, SQL, and ownership experience. However, missing must-have frontend development skills and top-tier engineering degree, plus no retail/design domain exposure. The role requires building brand-consistent frontends and working with CEO/design teams, which are unproven areas. Could be a stretch if the company is flexible on frontend for the right AI profile.
